---
title: "4.1.1 System software and application software"
subtitle: "Back-up, security, screensavers and device drivers · P153-P155"
format:
  clean-revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        <style>
        .reveal .term { color: #cf1f2e; font-weight: 700; }
        .reveal .mark { background: #fff36d; padding: 0 0.08em; }
        .reveal .tight-list li { margin-bottom: 0.24em; }
        .reveal .figure-note { font-size: 0.82em; line-height: 1.28; }
        .reveal .figure-placeholder { border: 1px dashed #8aa0bf; border-radius: 8px; background: #f4f8ff; color: #40526b; padding: 1.1em 1.3em; margin: 0.8em 0; font-size: 0.9em; text-align: center; }
        .reveal .clean-table table { font-size: 0.74em; line-height: 1.1; }
        .reveal .clean-table th, .reveal .clean-table td { padding: 0.24em 0.34em; vertical-align: top; }
        </style>
lang: zh-CN
---

## Course Directory

### Return to the main outline

[← Back to Unit 4 Directory / 返回 Unit 4 目录](../../index.html)

## Back-up Software

### Back-up utility

The operating system back-up utility can:

::: {.tight-list}
- allow a schedule for backing up files to be made
- only carry out a back-up procedure if there have been changes made to a file
:::

For total security there should be three versions of a file: the working version, a local back-up copy and a remote back-up version.

## Back-up Software

### Restore points and time-based backups

Back-up utilities can also:

::: {.tight-list}
- restore data, files or the computer from the back-up
- create a <span class="term">restore point</span>
- choose where back-up files are saved
:::

Examples in the textbook include Windows <span class="term">File History</span> and Mac OS <span class="term">Time Machine</span>.

## Back-up Software

### Figure 4.6

::: {.figure-placeholder}
Figure 4.6 Time machine message on Mac OS screenshot placeholder
:::

## Security Software

### Managing access and protection

<span class="term">Security software</span> is an over-arching utility that:

::: {.tight-list}
- manages access control and user accounts
- links into other utility software, such as virus checkers and spyware checkers
- protects network interfaces, for example through firewalls
- uses encryption and decryption so intercepted data is meaningless without a decryption key
- oversees software updating
:::

## Screensavers

### Original purpose and modern use

<span class="term">Screensavers</span> supply moving and still images on the monitor screen after a period of inactivity.

They were originally developed to protect older CRT monitors from phosphor burn. With modern LCD and OLED screens, this problem no longer exists.

Many screensavers are also used as part of the computer's security system.

## Screensavers

### Idle-state tasks

Some screensavers can activate useful background tasks that can only go on when the computer is in an idle state.

Examples include:

::: {.tight-list}
- virus scans
- distributed computing applications
:::

## Device Drivers

### Connecting hardware to the operating system

<span class="term">Device drivers</span> communicate with the operating system and translate data into a format understood by a hardware peripheral device.

Without device drivers:

::: {.tight-list}
- a hardware device would be unable to work with a computer
- a message such as <span class="mark">device not recognised</span> could appear
- when a USB device is plugged in, the operating system looks for the appropriate device driver
:::

## Device Drivers

### USB descriptors

All USB device drivers contain information about devices called <span class="term">descriptors</span>.

Descriptors allow the USB bus to ask a newly connected device what it is. They include:

::: {.tight-list}
- <span class="term">vendor id (VID)</span>
- <span class="term">product id (PID)</span>
- unique serial numbers
:::

If a device has no serial number, the operating system will treat it as new every time it is plugged into a USB port.

## Classroom Check

### Explain support utilities and drivers

A complete answer should include:

::: {.tight-list}
- back-up software can schedule backups and restore data
- three secure file versions are working, local back-up and remote back-up
- security software manages access, firewalls, encryption and updates
- screensavers can support security and idle-state tasks
- device drivers translate data for hardware peripheral devices
- USB descriptors include VID, PID and serial numbers
:::

## Bridge

### Next: Operating systems

The next topic explains the role and functions of an operating system.

## End

### Return to the main outline

[← Back to Unit 4 Directory / 返回 Unit 4 目录](../../index.html)
